en: You then, my child, be strengthened by the grace that is in Christ Jesus,
zh: 所以，我兒啊，你應當在基督耶穌的恩典裡剛強起來，

en: and what you have heard from me in the presence of many witnesses entrust to faithful men who will be able to teach others also.
zh: 又應當把你在許多見證人面前從我這裡聽見的，交託給那些又忠心又能夠教導別人的人。

en: Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
zh: 你應當和我同受磨難，好像基督耶穌的精兵。

en: No soldier gets entangled in civilian pursuits, since his aim is to please the one who enlisted him.
zh: 當兵的人不讓世務纏身，為要使那招兵的人歡喜。

en: An athlete is not crowned unless he competes according to the rules.
zh: 競賽的人若不遵守規則，就不能得冠冕。

en: It is the hard-working farmer who ought to have the first share of the crops.
zh: 勞力的農夫理當先嘗果實。

en: Think over what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in everything.
zh: 你要想想我的話，因為凡事主必給你領悟力。

en: Remember Jesus Christ, risen from the dead, the offspring of David, as preached in my gospel,
zh: 你要記得那從死人中復活的耶穌基督，他是大衛的後裔所生的，這就是我所傳的福音。

en: for which I am suffering, bound with chains as a criminal. But the word of God is not bound!
zh: 我為了這福音受了磨難，甚至像犯人一樣被捆綁起來；可是， 神的話卻不被捆綁。

en: Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect, that they also may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus with eternal glory.
zh: 因此，我為了選民忍受一切，好叫他們也可以得著在基督耶穌裡的救恩，和永遠的榮耀。

en: The saying is trustworthy, for: If we have died with him, we will also live with him;
zh: “我們若與基督同死，就必與他同活；

en: if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he also will deny us;
zh: 我們若能堅忍，就必與他一同作王； 我們若不認他，他必不認我們；

en: if we are faithless, he remains faithful- for he cannot deny himself.
zh: 我們縱然不信，他仍然是信實的， 因為他不能否定自己。” 這話是可信的。

en: Remind them of these things, and charge them before God not to quarrel about words, which does no good, but only ruins the hearers.
zh: 你要在 神面前把這些事提醒眾人，叮囑他們不要作無益的爭辯；這只能敗壞聽見的人。

en: Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly handling the word of truth.
zh: 你應當竭力在 神面前作一個蒙稱許、無愧的工人，正確地講解真理的道。

en: But avoid irreverent babble, for it will lead people into more and more ungodliness,
zh: 總要遠避世俗的空談，因為這些必會引人進到更不敬虔的地步。

en: and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
zh: 他們的話好像毒瘤一樣蔓延；他們當中有許米乃和腓理徒。

en: who have swerved from the truth, saying that the resurrection has already happened. They are upsetting the faith of some.
zh: 他們偏離了真道，說復活的事已經過去了，於是毀壞了一些人的信心。

en: But God's firm foundation stands, bearing this seal: "The Lord knows those who are his," and, "Let everyone who names the name of the Lord depart from iniquity."
zh: 然而， 神堅固的根基已經立定，上面刻著這樣的印：“主認識屬於他的人”和“凡稱呼主名的人都應當離開不義”。

en: Now in a great house there are not only vessels of gold and silver but also of wood and clay, some for honorable use, some for dishonorable.
zh: 在富貴人的家裡 ，不但有金器、銀器，也有木器、瓦器；有貴重的，也有卑賤的。

en: Therefore, if anyone cleanses himself from what is dishonorable, he will be a vessel for honorable use, set apart as holy, useful to the master of the house, ready for every good work.
zh: 人若自潔，離開卑賤的事，就必作貴重的器皿，成為聖潔，合主使用，預備行各樣的善事。

en: So flee youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
zh: 你應當逃避年輕人的私慾，要和那些以清潔的心求告主的人，一同追求公義、信心、愛心、和平。

en: Have nothing to do with foolish, ignorant controversies; you know that they breed quarrels.
zh: 你要拒絕愚蠢無知的問難；你知道這些事會引起爭論。

en: And the Lord's servant must not be quarrelsome but kind to everyone, able to teach, patiently enduring evil,
zh: 但主的僕人卻不可爭論，總要待人溫和，善於教導，存心忍耐，

en: correcting his opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant them repentance leading to a knowledge of the truth,
zh: 以溫柔勸導那些對抗的人，或許 神給他們悔改的心，可以認識真理。

en: and they may escape from the snare of the devil, after being captured by him to do his will.
zh: 他們雖然曾經被魔鬼擄去，隨從他的意思而行，或許也能醒悟過來，脫離魔鬼的陷阱。
